reviews.review_title: Game never loads! (Not just me either)
reviews.review_description:
I’m genuinely frustrated with this game.

The game simply does not work. It gets stuck on the “loading shaders” screen for an excessive amount of time (20–40 minutes or more) and never properly loads. I’ve tried all the obvious fixes—reinstalling multiple times, restarting the headset, and ensuring everything else is working correctly—and nothing changes.

What makes this worse is that it’s not just me. Others are clearly experiencing the same issue, as my friend downloaded the game at the same time on his quest and he can't load the game either! which strongly suggests this is a problem with the game itself, not individual devices.

Every other VR game I own loads instantly without issue, so this stands out as a poorly optimised or broken release.

At the end of the day, I paid for a product that I cannot use. That’s not acceptable. Until this is fixed, I cannot recommend this game to anyone. I would love to play this game so I'll wait for a developer to reply to this review with some information that might help me but if I don't get a reply in the next week I will have to get a refund. 

Fix the shader loading issue and I’ll happily reconsider my rating and play the game as I bought it to play—but right now, I feel like I have been robbed.

developer_response.body:
Hello Kingsativa420

We are truly sorry about the issue you have had its to do with the drivers for the hardware being updated, we have been working on an update for the game so missed this update to the drivers. We have addressed the issue and pushed a fix through so if you redownload the game it will be working as intended.

Please try it again as we would like to correct your 1 star review and the MR feature is really worth trying making our game unique.

Best wishes
The Starlight team

reviews.review_description:
Hi dev team!
First of all, thank you for this game — I’m having a lot of fun with it, especially creating courses and playing them with my daughter.

What I really like:

- Passthrough support is awesome and really adds to the experience.
- Lots of objects to build custom courses, which makes the editor fun and creative.
- Very kid-friendly: my 4-year-old daughter can play the courses I create, and that’s honestly a big win.

What I’d love to see improved:

- Push environmental interaction further.
I’d love to be able to build a course anywhere in my living room and use scanned real-world objects as actual obstacles (furniture, walls, etc.), not just virtual ones.

- Play area limits feel too small.
Instead of fully using the room scan, the game seems to create an approximate guardian based on it. This really limits creativity and prevents using the full space.

- A simple floor-level hole option.
Right now, holes are always attached to a platform, which forces them to be elevated. I’d love a “basic hole” that I can place directly on the floor anywhere in my room.

- More tee pad options.
For example, having an elevated tee pad would open up more interesting course designs.

- Copy / paste in edit mode.
This would be a huge quality-of-life improvement when building more complex courses.

Overall, I’m genuinely enjoying creating courses and playing them with my daughter. The foundation is already really strong — these changes would just push creativity and immersion even further.

Thanks for the great work, and keep it coming!

developer_response.body:
First off, Happy New Year to you! 

Secondly, thank you for our valuable feedback. We are currently working on some updates and you'll see these very shortly.

reviews.review_title: I Love this "House of Golf" Game! 💚⛳
reviews.review_description: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐As someone who grew up playing miniature golf as a kid on a boardwalk, I absolutely love this game. Great graphics and obstacles galore... so many mini golf games have gotten away from the fun windmills, ramps, moving objects, etc, that this game really hones in on, and it really hits home for me 😊. The virtual courses are amazing, but I must say that I am a little stumped how to keep the game locked into a mode where I can keep creating & playing in mixed reality mood. Currently, I've done a few tournament courses, only sometimes hitting par. The game gives me temporary access to the mixed reality mood, but when I log back in later, the option is gone. Developer, can you give me some more insight here?

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback and we are glad you're enjoying our game. We are making more updates which will go live very shortly. 

In terms of MR, the feature in the main menu to the left which shows a house and this allows you back into MR any time you like.

Please do drop us a note on our socials so we can look into any issues in more detail. Thanks once again.

reviews.review_description: Many people will buy this game if it supports Amvr golf clubs, just like walkabaut minigolf, the ball physics still leaves something to be desired, and the sounds too. but if it gets these, the 5 stars will rain down! Please add Hungarian subtitles. If it gets all of these, I will change it to 5 stars. But nice work so far!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the feedback. We are currently evaluating the Amrv and also looking at updated and additional options in the future. Thank you so much for your review and comments.

reviews.review_title: Not walkabout but worth a look.
reviews.review_description: This is nothing on walkabout mini golf which should always have your time and money however there are some good things to note. The art style is like house flipper games not a bad thing, music is good and MR to build your own little course is really good and easy to do. The down sides are hitting the ball and ball physics don't feel nearly half as good as walkabout or golf+ for that matter. Tutorial stopped working the first time round and drove me made. All in all good effort, but work to do.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are working towards improvements in all areas and offering MR mode allows us to introduce some creative elements which we're glad you're enjoying.

reviews.review_description: I really want to like this game. The idea is really great. I will start off by saying the VR mode works well. The only issue I find is the hole size is a hair small. The ball doesn't go in sometimes even inches from the hole. Thats a little frustrating. I may  have missed it in the menus, but I'm not sure if one controller can be disabled. Having to hold both is cumbersome. As far as mixed reality mode, for me it's completely unplayable. No matter what I do, the objects don't align with my floor. They are either floating above the floor or below the floor. I wanted to play this exclusively for the mixed reality. In it's current state, its difficult to enjoy considering I can't play the mode I purchased it for. I hope there is a fix or update in the near future to address some of these problems. I gave this 3 stars mainly for it's creative approach and potential.

developer_response.body:
Good morning, and thank you for taking the time to review our game. Mixed Reality does require some initial setup. When creating a new level, choose any room you’d like and run a full environment scan. Be sure to walk around the space to capture all surfaces and objects accurately.

One important detail is to verify your boundary settings. These are usually already configured, but if not, please make sure the floor level is correctly detected during the initial scan. This step is essential for the experience to function properly.

We’ll be releasing a setup tutorial video this week to help guide this process visually.

Also, note that you do not need to use the second controller to play.

We hope you’re able to get everything working smoothly. As you may have seen on social media, many players have been able to build great mixed reality environment. It all comes down to the initial setup.

reviews.review_description:
Really fun and relaxing game. It’s nice how creative you can be with building your own courses in the mixed reality mode. You can bounce the ball off surfaces, walls, ceilings and experiment with ramps and hazards which is cool 

It’s simple to use and there’s a good balance of courses, challenges and casual play too. Highly worth⭐️

developer_response.body: Thank you for your valuable feedback.

reviews.review_description: Guys: I get maybe these are passion projects, but you have to at least play the game before you release it on the app store. Why are the controls for your watch ON THE HAND the watch is on (please tell me you've worn a watch before). And whose idea was is to have the watch switch hands so easily? Why do you have such a complex system for handling and dropping a ball? Just put the ball in the starting area. There's also tutorial bug -- it literally can't exist past "the golf club will not exist if you're not holding down a button (I don't know why this is)" -- the club won't interact with the ball, button or not. How did you get this close and so so so far. What in the world.

developer_response.body:
Good morning. Just to clarify a few things, so thanks for taking the time to post here. 

In regards to the watch on the thumbstick. We tell players at the start of the Tutorial to use a Single Controller. Rather than confuse the issue and have the wrist menu operate with a second controller pointer, we allow them to stick with the single controller approach and use the thumb stick to select options. With the infrequent use of the wrist menu, we felt this was a satisfactory resolution given the instruction we had already given the players about using a single controller.

Switching active controller easily:
This was to make it as simple as possible for left or right-handed players to easily swap active controller. If you play with a single controller, like the tutorial says, it’s less of an issue if you’ve found yourself accidentally tapping the trigger on the other controller.

Ball placement:
Being able to choose your tee shot is important for optimal shot strategy. The best scores are often led by the best tee shot. So rather than force the players location, we let them choose where on the Tee Zone they start the level from.

Active Club Bug:
We will investigate this issue immediately as this the first report, but extremely important we look into this straight away.

Active Club Status:
The club having an active status and inactive status, is to stop the frustration of accidentally connecting with the ball after you’ve teleported. In testing, we found that players weren’t happy about accidentally causing a shot with the active club, immediately after they have teleported near to the ball. 

This solution was one way to make sure the club is not producing unwanted collisions, which were even more frustrating because they added a shot to the players shot count. 

Thanks

reviews.review_description:
House of Golf VR takes mini golf to a whole new level of immersion, blending charm, challenge, and cutting-edge tech into one unforgettable experience. The courses are bursting with color and creativity, from cozy living rooms to wild, toy-filled tracks, and every putt feels satisfying thanks to the spot-on physics and responsive controls.

But the real game-changer is the Mixed Reality (MR) feature. Being able to transform your own living space into a mini golf course is nothing short of magical. You can line up your shots around your coffee table, bank the ball off your real walls, and watch as the digital and physical worlds merge seamlessly. It’s intuitive, wildly fun, and showcases just how far VR gaming has evolved.

Whether you’re a casual player or a hardcore golf enthusiast, House of Golf VR delivers pure joy, precision gameplay, and endless replay value. It’s not just mini golf, it’s your home turned into the ultimate putting playground.

In short - A visually delightful, technically brilliant, and genuinely groundbreaking MR experience. A must-have for every Meta Quest library.

Would love to see one handed mode as your second hand serves no purpose! Also a better movement method

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your delightful review.

reviews.review_description: I downloaded this game on day one. I looked at the reviews, and feel taht i must write my first review ever. I honestly don't know where the negative reviews are coming from. This game is worth the investment. I play other games with attachments, and I would love this option, but for now it keeps everyone on par on the leaderboards. What's wrong with that? I do find the holes annoyingly small compared to the ball size and physics, but this game is worthy of a true 5 star review. I hope the devs are proud and working towards making the hole bigger. Well worth the purchase price, especially if the devs have some ongoing commitment.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the positive review. We are working on updates for the future and we are glad you are enjoying the game.

reviews.review_description: Such a charming, relaxing mini-golf experience that really exceeds my expectations. The controls feel natural, i had no problems with the wrist menu like one of the other reviewers. Great way to unwind in VR, I hope they are planning a multiplayer addition. Love it so far!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your wonderful feedback. We will share our roadmap soon of updates.

reviews.review_description: The graphics are really nice, and it's got a really lovely vibe. It's simple to pick up and in mixed reality pretty much anyone can play.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your wonderful feedback. We are working on new updates that will be included soon.
